section.heading: Preservation of electronic messages and other records

subsection.heading: Regulations Required.—
subsection.chapeau: The Archivist shall promulgate regulations governing Federal agency preservation of electronic messages that are determined to be records. Such regulations shall, at a minimum—

paragraph.content: require the electronic capture, management, and preservation of such electronic records in accordance with the records disposition requirements of chapter 33;
require that such electronic records are readily accessible for retrieval through electronic searches; and
include timelines for Federal agency implementation of the regulations that ensure compliance as expeditiously as practicable.

subsection.heading: Coverage of Other Electronic Records.—
subsection.content: To the extent practicable, the regulations promulgated under subsection (a) shall also include requirements for the capture, management, and preservation of other electronic records.

subsection.heading: Review of Regulations Required.—
subsection.content: The Archivist shall periodically review and, as necessary, amend the regulations promulgated under subsection (a).

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 116–283, div. H, title XCVI, § 9602(b)(1), Jan. 1, 2021, 134 Stat. 4828.)

note.heading: Deadline for Regulations
note.p: Pub. L. 116–283, div. H, title XCVI, § 9602(b)(2), Jan. 1, 2021, 134 Stat. 4828, provided that: “Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act [Jan. 1, 2021], the Archivist shall propose the regulations required under section 2912(a) of title 44, United States Code, as added by paragraph (1).”
